mappings.rationaleAI: Broad coverage of classical mechanics including force, matter, energy, and gravitational waves relevant to A (principles of classical mechanics).
Directly covers Newton's law of universal gravitation (A.3.b.v), Kepler's laws of planetary motion (A.3.b.vi), and celestial mechanics (B).
Directly covers relativistic mechanics (C), including special relativity, relativistic kinematics, and the equivalence of mass and energy (A.3.e.iii).
Directly covers fluid statics, hydrodynamics, aerodynamics, laminar and turbulent flow, and compressible flow throughout section F.
Covers the historical development of mechanics from Galileo and Newton through Einstein, providing context for sections A, B, and C.
Directly relevant to Newton's laws of motion (A.3.b.i), law of universal gravitation (A.3.b.v), and the foundations of celestial mechanics (B).
Foundational to kinematics (A.3.a), projectile motion and ballistics (A.3.b.iii), and the law of inertia (A.3.b.i).
Covers stress-strain relationships, hardness, and mechanical properties of materials relevant to elasticity (D) and yield strength and fracture (G.3).
Covers rheological phenomena including viscoelastic deformation and flow, constitutive equations, and soft material mechanics relevant to sections D and G.
Covers mathematical formulations underlying mechanics including differential equations relevant to Lagrange's and Hamilton's equations (A.6.d).
Relevant to nonlinear dynamics in complex mechanical systems (A.6), sensitivity to initial conditions in celestial mechanics (B), and turbulent flow (F.3.b).
Covers fundamental concepts of matter and mass central to the parameters of classical mechanics (A.1) and the equivalence of mass and energy (A.3.e.iii).
Covers philosophical foundations of space, time, motion, and statistical mechanics relevant to the conceptual framework of classical mechanics (A.1).
Directly covers types of vibrational waves, their properties and modes of propagation (E.3), and wave behaviour including diffraction and interference.
Covers acoustics, frequency, resonance, and the Doppler effect directly relevant to vibrations of elastic bodies (E) and detection of vibrations (E.5).
Covers statics of beams and bridges (A.2), stress dynamics of elastic materials (D), and yield strength and fracture of structural materials (G.3).
Covers practical applications of gravity, electromagnetism, and mechanical principles relevant to statics (A.2) and dynamics (A.3) in engineering contexts.
Covers angular momentum, Newtonian mechanics, and relativity relevant to celestial mechanics (B) and relativistic mechanics (C).
Relevant to celestial mechanics (B) including planetary orbits, Kepler's laws (A.3.b.vi), and gravitational perturbations (B.2).

mappings.vsiAuthor: David George Bowers, Emyr Martyn Roberts
mappings.rationaleAI: Covers gravitational mechanics of the Earth-Moon system relevant to celestial mechanics (B) and fluid dynamics of ocean tides relevant to fluid mechanics (F).
Covers calculus, differentiation, and integration that form the mathematical language of mechanics, essential to Lagrange's and Hamilton's equations (A.6.d).
Covers the historical development of celestial mechanics (B.1) including the work of Kepler and Newton on planetary motion.
Relevant to celestial mechanics (B) including planetary orbits, the n-body problem (B.4), and gravitational perturbations in planetary systems (B.2).
